scenario: useReducer vs useState for complex form state with 12 fields.
context: Form with conditional fields, derived values, validation state.
sam_initial_position: useReducer. Centralized transitions, easier to test.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 12 useState calls is verbose but readable. Each field's logic local.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For cross-field rules (derived value from 3 fields), useReducer's centralized state simplifies.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Use form library (RHF) - it's purpose-built.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For custom forms beyond RHF's model, useReducer is the fallback.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Agreed. RHF default, useReducer for custom.

context_dependent_answer: Forms: RHF. Complex non-form state: useReducer. Simple: useState.
decision_framework: debate-151: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: useReducer vs useState for complex form state with 12 fields.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors useState for everything. Senior insight: state shape matters. Reducers for complex transitions, state libraries for shared state.

scenario: Headless component pattern (state-only hook + headless components) vs styled component pattern (everything bundled).
context: Internal design system used by 5 teams.
alex_initial_position: Headless + styled wrapper. Two layers: primitives (state) and styled (defaults). Teams can use either.
sam_initial_position: Styled only. Headless is over-engineering. One layer = one mental model.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Headless layer enables custom styling for marketing pages without forking.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For 5 teams, divergence is the enemy. One styled component = consistency.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Headless underneath is documented escape hatch. Doesn't fragment unless used.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Styled default + headless under.

context_dependent_answer: 5-team design system: styled + headless underneath. Single product: styled only.
decision_framework: debate-152: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Headless component pattern (state-only hook + headless components) vs styled component pattern (everything bundled).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors pick one. Senior insight: layered API serves both consistency and flexibility.

scenario: Schema validation library: Zod vs Valibot vs Yup.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Zod has zod-to-* ecosystem (forms, OpenAPI, JSON Schema). Worth its size.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Valibot 13KB vs Zod 50KB. Mobile-first matters.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Zod tree-shakes well. Practical bundle delta is smaller.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Valibot wins tree-shaking by design - functional API.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted on bundle. Zod's ecosystem still leads.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For most apps, ecosystem matters more. Zod.

context_dependent_answer: Most apps: Zod. Mobile-bundle-critical: Valibot. Legacy: Yup.
decision_framework: debate-153: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Schema validation library: Zod vs Valibot vs Yup.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors validate manually. Senior insight: schemas give runtime + types from one source.

scenario: Suspense for data fetching vs traditional isLoading flags.
context: App on React 19, considering Suspense for queries.
sam_initial_position: isLoading. More control over loading UI.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Suspense boundaries handle loading at the right level. Less prop drilling for isLoading.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For granular UI (only this section loading), isLoading more flexible.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Per-component Suspense boundary gives same granularity.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Suspense throws Promise - debugging harder.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React 19 + Suspense is mature. DevTools handle it.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Suspense for new code, isLoading for compatibility.

context_dependent_answer: React 19+: Suspense. React 18: isLoading or Suspense with care.
decision_framework: debate-154: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Suspense for data fetching vs traditional isLoading flags.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors track isLoading manually. Senior insight: Suspense + Promise integration eliminates boilerplate.

scenario: Stylex (Meta) vs Tailwind for new project. Stylex is type-safe, atomic, zero-runtime.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tailwind has IntelliSense, plugins, mature.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Stylex is type-safe by design. Tokens are values.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tailwind v4 + theme variables achieves same.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Stylex compiles to atomic CSS like Tailwind but from JS objects.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Stylex ecosystem smaller. Risk.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Meta-backed reduces risk.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For most teams: Tailwind. Stylex for Meta stacks.

context_dependent_answer: Most: Tailwind. Meta stack: Stylex. Cutting edge: try Stylex.
decision_framework: debate-155: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: Stylex (Meta) vs Tailwind for new project. Stylex is type-safe, atomic, zero-runtime.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors pick by hype. Senior insight: ecosystem maturity > theoretical advantages.

scenario: View Transitions API for SPA routing transitions vs Framer Motion AnimatePresence.
alex_initial_position: View Transitions. Browser-native, zero JS for the animation.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: View Transitions hand animation to compositor. AnimatePresence runs on main thread.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Customization via View Transitions is CSS-only. Limited.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 90% of route transitions (fade, slide), CSS is enough.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For element-shared transitions (image expand on navigation), needs JS coordination.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: View Transitions natively support shared element via view-transition-name.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. View Transitions for routes, Framer for complex.

context_dependent_answer: SPA routes: View Transitions. In-component: Framer.
decision_framework: debate-156: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: View Transitions API for SPA routing transitions vs Framer Motion AnimatePresence.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors animate in JS. Senior insight: browser-native (View Transitions) skips JS when possible.

scenario: Mutation testing (Stryker) vs coverage thresholds for quality gates.
alex_initial_position: Mutation testing. Coverage 100% doesn't mean tests catch bugs.
sam_initial_position: Coverage as floor + manual review. Mutation testing is slow.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Mutation testing catches tests that pass without asserting.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Stryker runs are hours. Slow CI.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Run on schedule (nightly), not per-PR.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Nightly catches drift. Acceptable.

context_dependent_answer: Critical: coverage + nightly mutation. Standard: coverage.
decision_framework: debate-157: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Mutation testing (Stryker) vs coverage thresholds for quality gates.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors chase 100% coverage. Senior insight: coverage is necessary but insufficient. Tests should catch mutations.

scenario: Generic over-engineering: when does TypeScript get too clever?
context: Helper utilities with deeply generic types.
alex_initial_position: Keep generics shallow. 1-2 type params max.
sam_initial_position: Generics as needed. Library code may need more.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 5-param generics are unreadable. Errors are cryptic.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For type-safe builders (form, query), generics are necessary.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted for libraries. Application code: shallow.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: App code: 1-2 generics max. Library: as needed but documented.
decision_framework: debate-158: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: Generic over-engineering: when does TypeScript get too clever?. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors avoid generics. Senior insight: balance. Generics for reuse; too many for cleverness.

scenario: Concurrent rendering with useTransition: when worth it?
context: App with heavy update on user action (filter, sort large list).
sam_initial_position: useTransition for measured slow paths only.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Wrapping cheap updates in transition is harmless.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Transition has subtle semantics - non-urgent updates can be interrupted. Bugs from misuse.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For interactive updates marked transition, React interrupts cleanly.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Use transition where you measured the bottleneck. Otherwise default.

context_dependent_answer: Measured slow updates: transition. Fast: default.
decision_framework: debate-159: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Concurrent rendering with useTransition: when worth it?. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mix urgent + heavy updates. Senior insight: transitions separate UI responsiveness from heavy work.

scenario: Vitest vs Jest for new project.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Vitest's Vite integration means same config as build. One transform pipeline.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Jest has more matchers, plugins.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Vitest API is Jest-compatible. Migration trivial.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For new project: Vitest. For Jest existing: stay.

context_dependent_answer: Vite stack: Vitest. CRA legacy: Jest.
decision_framework: debate-160: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Vitest vs Jest for new project.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use whatever's bundled. Senior insight: test runner matters for daily DX.

scenario: Documenting component variants: Storybook stories vs MDX vs auto-generated.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Stories are tested + documented. One source.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Auto-docs are bland. MDX better for explanations.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Autodocs + MDX where needed. Layered.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Library: autodocs + MDX prose where needed.
decision_framework: debate-161: judge the component library strategy debate in this scenario: Documenting component variants: Storybook stories vs MDX vs auto-generated.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.

scenario: Error boundaries with recovery vs error boundaries that escalate.
context: App with various error types - some recoverable, some not.
alex_initial_position: Classify errors, recover when possible, escalate when not.
sam_initial_position: Always escalate to user. Auto-recovery hides bugs.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Network blips are recoverable. Don't show error UI for them.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Silent recovery hides patterns. User unaware = no feedback to fix.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Telemetry captures. UI doesn't have to.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Recover network, escalate logic.

context_dependent_answer: Network: recover. Logic: escalate. Telemetry: always.
decision_framework: debate-162: judge the error boundary strategy debate in this scenario: Error boundaries with recovery vs error boundaries that escalate.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors show errors everywhere. Senior insight: error UX is design. Different errors need different responses.

scenario: Loadable components vs React.lazy for code splitting.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React 19's lazy supports SSR.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: With limitations. loadable handles streaming SSR better.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For most cases, React.lazy + Suspense.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for client-only. SSR-heavy: loadable.

context_dependent_answer: Client-only: React.lazy. SSR-heavy: loadable.
decision_framework: debate-163: judge the code splitting philosophy debate in this scenario: Loadable components vs React.lazy for code splitting.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't split. Senior insight: splitting is the difference between 50KB and 500KB initial bundle.

scenario: Live regions: polite vs assertive.
context: Toast notifications, validation errors, status changes.
alex_initial_position: Polite by default. Don't interrupt user.
sam_initial_position: Assertive for critical (errors). Polite for informational.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Assertive interrupts current speech. Disruptive.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For form errors that block submit, user needs to know immediately.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Form error inline + aria-invalid handles that without live region.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For toast errors (out-of-band), assertive justified.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Polite default, assertive for critical out-of-band.

where_they_agree: Inline errors don't need live region
context_dependent_answer: Polite default. Assertive: critical out-of-band only (error toasts, payment failures).
decision_framework: debate-164: judge the accessibility tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Live regions: polite vs assertive.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use assertive everywhere. Senior insight: SR users hate interruption. Polite respects flow.

scenario: Domain events: pub/sub library vs simple custom event bus.
context: App needs analytics events fired from many places.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 20 lines of pub/sub is trivial. No dep.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: mitt is 200 bytes. Includes typing.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 200 bytes, dep adds complexity. Custom is fine.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom = bespoke. Library = known pattern.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Custom event bus is the simplest TS pattern. New devs recognize.

context_dependent_answer: Custom 20-line bus or mitt - either. Don't reach for larger libs.
decision_framework: debate-165: judge the frontend architecture patterns debate in this scenario: Domain events: pub/sub library vs simple custom event bus.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors think pub/sub needs a library. Senior insight: pub/sub is 20 lines. Don't import what you can write in a coffee break.

scenario: Server-side state vs client-side state for filters/sorting.
context: Filter state in URL or in client state?
alex_initial_position: URL state. Bookmarkable, shareable, back/forward works.
sam_initial_position: Client state. Faster, no URL pollution.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: URL state means user shares 'this view'. Powerful UX.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: URL gets long with many filters.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Compressed URL state (LZ-string) keeps short.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Complex serialization. Client state simpler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For UX shareability, URL state wins. Use nuqs or similar library.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. URL state via library.

context_dependent_answer: User-facing filter views: URL. Internal UI state: client.
decision_framework: debate-166: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Server-side state vs client-side state for filters/sorting.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors keep filters in component state. Senior insight: URL is shareable state. Filter views in URL get bookmarks, shares, back-button for free.

scenario: Render props vs hooks for sharing component logic.
context: Sharing 'has user hovered' logic across components.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hooks compose. Render props pyramid.

debate.argument: Render props for class components.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: New code: hooks. Render props are 2018.

debate.argument: Stack supports complex back paths through branches.

scenario: GraphQL subscription vs WebSocket vs SSE for real-time.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Dashboard is one-way: server → client. SSE fits.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: WebSocket handles both directions. Future-proof.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: YAGNI bi-directional. SSE is HTTP, easier proxy/load-balancer.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. SSE for one-way.

context_dependent_answer: Server-push only: SSE. Chat/collab: WebSocket. GraphQL subscriptions: subscriptions-transport.

debate.speaker: sam

debate.argument: For 0ms timer, fires after current task. Sufficient.
